channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:summary: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations.

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [BC Gov News] Building volunteer fire department capacity and [Android Authority] Android Auto just made EV trip planning a lot smarter for these car brands, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How do information systems and governance structures in rural communities determine who has access to decision-making power, and whose interests do transparency measures actually serve? Topics include: Cougar alert systems and emergency communication infrastructure in rural BC, Canada Post mail delivery consolidation and loss of bundled community services, Aboriginal Title Transparency Act and strategic disclosure in Indigenous governance, Epistemic infrastructure and who controls information about community needs, Heiltsuk and other Indigenous-led information sovereignty models.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Building volunteer fire department capacity
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Android Auto just made EV trip planning ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] TRAILBLAZER CHRONICLES: Bathrooms, b...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Iran’s internet shutdown proves we need to go be...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Vehicle stolen in Chilliwack recovered...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Who Gets to Do Science?
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] MLA McInnis introduces Aboriginal Ti...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Potential cougar sighted in Centennial...

Additional sources provided:

  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Audio tapes challenge Milgram obedience findin...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Deadline looms for property owner to...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. moving to alter environment ass...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Prescribed burns set for Nemiah Vall...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I put Claude vs. Deepseek through 7 real-world ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Running a Game on a PC with no System RAM
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Inside the OpenAI project where freelance...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Anthropic accidentally exposed part of Cl...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Mercor says it is "one of thousands of companies" h...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] How AI has suddenly become much more useful to open-...
  • WIRED: [WIRED] Robotaxi Outage in China Leaves Passengers Stranded ...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] Robots, cameras, and lots of data about garbage: In...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Canada Post to convert remaining doo...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] Half of BC’s Community Paramedic Positions Are Unfilled and [Williams Lake Tribune] New Democrats pass resolution to protect northwest B.C. coastal tanker ban, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can the Cariboo build a working landscape that is economically viable, ecologically sustainable, and socially equitable when these three goals are often in tension? Topics include: BC Timber Sales policy design and competitive market dynamics in forestry, Soil health science, microbial inoculants, and measurement challenges for ranching, Carbon markets, ecosystem service payments, and aggregation models for smallholder operations, Indigenous co-governance, traditional ecological knowledge, and tenure restructuring, Infrastructure gaps: connectivity, processing facilities, long-term capital, and organizational capacity.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Half of BC’s Community Paramedic Positions Are Un...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] New Democrats pass resolution to pro...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake celebrates young piani...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Volunteers help local children facin...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Lhtako Dene Nation launches long-...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Doctors say regulatory decisions cou...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] BC Timber Sales to increase fibre access, stre...

Additional sources provided:

  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Tame the Tape: Open-Source Dotterboard for Bulk S...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] This Front Panel Makes Its Own Clean-Edged Drill ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Medieval Alhambra’s Pulser Pump and Other Aquatic...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I put ChatGPT vs Gemini through 7 real-world te...
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Google just made wireless emergency aler...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] PDP-11 Lives in Literal Computer Desk Once More
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] When Will Japan’s Cherry Blossoms Bloom? A...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] LIGO May Have Detected The First Primordial B...
  • CNET: [CNET] Newsom Signs Executive Order Requiring AI Companies t...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] I installed this Arch-based distro my way in under 5...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] New Alzheimer's Treatment Strategy Reverses C...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] What to Know About California’s Executive ...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] Holganix's Howard W. Buffett: agriculture 'way of s...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Augmented reality glasses can aid dementia pat...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [New Atlas] Ancient Roman board game stumped experts for decades – until AI played and [XDA Developers] I built a DIY automation keypad for under $20, and it replaced three devices on my desk, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling Topics include: data sovereignty, AI, infrastructure, community ownership, maintenance.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Ancient Roman board game stumped experts for dec...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I built a DIY automation keypad for under $...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] ALEXANDER: Aaron and Gail Bregg will b...
  • TechCrunch: [TechCrunch] Sora’s shutdown could be a reality check moment...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Firefox gets big update with built-in VPN, Split Vi...
  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Top devs share their placeholder art to prove ...

Additional sources provided:

  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] An interview with Andy Weir about the accuracy ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Writing an Open-World Engine for the Nintendo 64
  • Noahpinion: [Noahpinion] Maybe you should have bought an electric car
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Clever telescopic camper trailer reinvents a gam...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Training a Transformer with 1970s-era Technology
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Pig-boar hybrids in Fukushima evacuation zone re...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I revived my old Android phone by turning i...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Upside-down tiny house packs clever layout and e...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] The weirdest smart lighting gadget I never kne...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I stopped buying desk organizers once I rea...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Your smart TV is screenshotting everything ...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Stop buying hard drives based on capacity—chec...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Weekend work increased more than 40%': How AI i...
  • MacRumors: [MacRumors] Apple Reportedly Working on 'Small Sensor for Ma...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514

Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)

item.title: Resilient Rural Futures
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] Why strong authentication beyond the browser will define the future of connected devices and [How-To Geek] This is the fastest and cheapest way to build a fully offline Home Assistant smart home, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Resilient Rural Futures Topics include: broadband, connectivity, telemedicine, renewable energy, solar.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Why strong authentication beyond the browser wil...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] This is the fastest and cheapest way to build ...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Nuclear energy is the essential backbone for th...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Nvidia's Jensen Huang has a message for b...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I replaced my entire Proxmox backup strateg...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] The Narwhal’s investigation into contaminated ...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Off-grid tiny house is well-suited to full-time ...

Additional sources provided:

  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Creating a DIY homelab: Fast, free, and beginn...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] 7 household repairs that are cheaper and easie...
  • Android Police: [Android Police] Stop paying for cloud storage and get yours...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Your nearly full SSD is tanking your performan...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] Local Opposition Is Slowing A.I. Data Centers...
  • The Marginalian: [The Marginalian] Telling Is Listening: Ursula K. Le Guin on...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Marshall McLuhan Was Right About Claude, T...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] KaOS begins tearing systemd apart after dro...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Collagen Supplements May Offer Real Benefits,...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] BUXTON: Spring’s renewal is just aroun...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Playful ‘Space Dice’ Kit Shows Off Clever Design
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Fedora 44 will automatically make your Wind...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] This MCP Server turned my NAS into a self-h...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Watch Electricity Slosh: Visualizing Impedance Ma...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Good Samaritan killed at crash scene west of Williams Lake and [Williams Lake Tribune] Fuel management projects to begin north of Kamloops, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Cariboo Voices & Local News Topics include: broadband, satellite, connectivity, AI, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Good Samaritan killed at crash scene...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Fuel management projects to begin no...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Calgary author bringing Chinese Excl...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Man with child porn charge sought by...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Brain Pattern Reveals Why Chronic Pain Leads ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] TRU Board of Governors considering c...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] TRU delays decision on Williams Lake...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Quesnel café owner spends month baki...

Additional sources provided:

  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] ‘Big dogs, small dogs, baby dogs’...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Yellowhead Project manager overvi...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Professional Employees Association r...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Cadet Band marches into B.C. venues
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. seniors advocate continues to w...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Latest proposed changes to B.C.’s He...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Okanagan unhoused encampment injunct...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Fulmer brings BC Conservative leader...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Quesnel man sentenced to 30 months f...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Probation for customer who assaulted...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. launches new marine, coastal te...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Victoria trans community warns B.C. ...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-20250514
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Kelowna drone operator fined $5K for helicopter interference during 2023 wildfire and [The Narwhal] Decades in the making: Mi’kmaq and Parks Canada strike historic partnership in Nova Scotia, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life Topics include: satellite, connectivity, solar, data sovereignty, AI.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] Why Humans Still Burn Logs for Power, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Indigenous Lands & Innovation Topics include: renewable energy, solar, data sovereignty, AI, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Redevelopment project at Jubilee Place in Williams Lake adjusted following provincial budget and [100 Mile Free Press] Air quality monitors to be installed at Watch Lake VFD, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Community Tech & Governance Topics include: broadband, renewable energy, data sovereignty, AI, infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] Half of BC’s Community Paramedic Positions Are Unfilled and [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Fire Department responds to vehicle fire in Dairy Fields, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] Google unveils new ‘vibe design’ tool to help anyone design a high-fidelity UI using natural language and [Business Insider] This 16-year-old refused a $300,000 offer to drop out of high school and now runs his own AI company, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Deep dive on Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling Topics include: broadband, AI, infrastructure, funding, pilot project.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).
